title: CustomerSegmentationTypeValues
description: Customer segmentation involves categorizing customers into different groups based on shared characteristics

x-enumeration-literals.name: Demographic Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Age
Gender
Income
Education
Occupation
Family size
Marital status
Ethnicity

x-enumeration-literals.name: Geographic Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Country
Region
City
Neighborhood
Climate
Urban vs. rural

x-enumeration-literals.name: Psychographic Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Lifestyle
Values
Personality
Interests
Opinions
Social status

x-enumeration-literals.name: Behavioral Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Purchasing behavior
Spending habits
User status (e.g., first-time vs. regular users)
Loyalty
Benefits sought
Occasion (e.g., holiday vs. everyday use)
Usage rate

x-enumeration-literals.name: Technographic Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Technology usage patterns
Preferred devices (e.g., smartphones, tablets)
Software and applications used
Level of tech savviness
Online behavior

x-enumeration-literals.name: Firmographic Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Industry
Company size
Revenue
Number of employees
Location
Company structure

x-enumeration-literals.name: Needs-based Segmentation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specific customer needs or problems that the product/service addresses
Desired benefits
Product features valued by the customer
